title: Marcgraviastrum cuneifolium
intro: Marcgraviastrum cuneifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Marcgraviaceae, also known as the clammy vine. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, due to its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Marcgraviastrum cuneifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.

This family is part of the order Ericales, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that also includes heath, blueberry, and chocolate. The order Ericales is part of the clade asterids, a major group of flowering plants that also includes the orders gentianales and lamiales. Marcgraviastrum cuneifolium is a species of flowering plant in the genus Marcgraviastrum, which is endemic to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Marcgraviastrum is a small group of epiphytic plants that are related to the more well-known genus Marcgravia.
